helpful: fedcourts paths resolved the snapshot/event/outcome locations cleanly
the snapshot exposed docket_number (IFP range) and appeal_from, which carry most of the predictive signal for a cert disposition
gaps: fedcourts query returns opinion-level disposition labels (other/dismissed) rather than a cert grant/deny outcome, so it gives no usable petition-disposition base rate for scotus petitions
no way to filter priors to 'petition' events specifically (IFP vs paid docket, cert granted vs denied) to compute a calibrated grant rate
notes: Prediction rested on the snapshot's structural features plus well-known public SCOTUS certiorari statistics; the corpus query could not supply a cert-specific prior. A petition-disposition-aware prior (grant rate by docket type) would materially improve calibration.
